---
title: Vipassana Course
date: 2025-09-14 10:23:45
author: Apurva Nakade
categories: ['philosophy', 'meditation', 'mindfullness', 'retreat']
---

In summer of 2025 I attended a 10-day Vipassana course in [Delaware](https://pubbananda.dhamma.org/). This has been a life-changing experience for me. This post contains some of the things I learned in the course. 

## the three types of wisdoms
S. N. Goenka in his talks, emphasizes the difference between "intellectual wisdom" (cintā-maya-paññā) and "experiential wisdom" (bhāvanā-maya-paññā). That true wisdom is one that is obtained by experiencing something yourself 

I see this all the time in my students

## the 5 precepts

- do not kill 
- do not lie 
- do not steal 
- no sexual misconduct
- no intoxicants

## strong determination 

- strong will

## gratitude and compassion
- being selfless 

- waking up early, giving up addictions
- strong determination 
- clear mind 



<p style="text-align:center">
![](https://www.dhamma.org/assets/aniwheel-a87352d07ff79e922e5afbc8775f603b.gif)</p>
